communications.subject: FW: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: RFP List
communications.communication:
Good afternoon:

This is in response to your email correspondence below. Please be advised that the City construes your request for records as a request coming under New Hampshire’s Right-to-Know law, RSA chapter 91-A.
Please be further advised that the City has no obligation to respond to requests from out-of-state corporations unless the corporation can show that it is located and/or has a nexus in New Hampshire. See McBurney v. Young, 569 U.S. 221 (2013). The City receives a large number of requests from out-of-state data collection corporations requesting financial and other reports to be submitted electronically to an email address and/or website. Due to the significant amount of time it takes for staff to research and generate reports in response to the out-of-state requests from data collection companies, the City will not transmit responses to such requests.

Please note that the City is required to make documents which are subject to disclosure, accessible for public review on its regular business premises (RSA Chapter 91-A:4,I). In accordance with RSA 91-A, the City would provide you, or a Representative (Mr. Van Fleet), the opportunity to personally review records, free of charge, at City Hall.  To the extent that your Representative would request copies of any documents in electronic format, your Representative may bring a sealed/unopened thumb drive and the City will upload the documents to the thumb drive, free of charge. If your Representative does not have a sealed/unopened thumb drive at the time of the scheduled visit to City Hall, we have new thumb drives that may be purchased at cost price.

Please contact our office at 603-225-8505 to schedule a date and time for you to review the documents at City Hall. The City will require approximately thirty (30) days prior notice of the date of when you intend to review the documents so that we can have the documents ready for your review upon your arrival.

Notwithstanding the aforementioned, please be advised that you may find information you are seeking on the City’s website at https://www.concordnh.gov/1092/Bids-Proposals-Quotations.

Thank you,

Velinda Diaz
Paralegal

City of Concord
Legal Department
41 Green Street, Concord, NH 03301
(603) 225-8505
vdiaz@concordnh.gov<mailto:vdiaz@concordnh.gov>

The information contained in this electronic message and any attachments to this message may contain confidential and/or privileged information and is intended for the exclusive use of the addressee(s). title: Washington County Public Schools Open Records Request - Student enrollment data

communications.subject: Maryland Public Information Act Request: Washington County Public Schools Open Records Request - Student enrollment data
communications.communication:
Dear Open Records Coordinator,

I am writing on behalf of the Data Acquisition Lab to formally request access to public records under the Maryland Public Information Act, Title 4. We are requesting aggregated counts of student enrollment at the most granular geographic level available, which will enable a more meaningful understanding of community-level trends. We plan to request this information on a bi-annual basis going forward.

I am specifically requesting a tabular dataset containing the counts of enrolled students in the Washington County Public Schools for the current (2025-2026) school year, disaggregated by the following fields:
- School name
- United States Census Tract
- Grade level (Pre-K through 12, or the finest granularity available for each school)

Please provide this data in electronic format (CSV preferred), including the following fields where available:
- School identifier (name, NCES ID)
- Census Tract GEOID (full 11-digit or abbreviated 6-digit code)
- Grade level
- Student enrollment count

Privacy Considerations:
- This request does not seek, nor does it require, personally identifiable information (PII) or any data points that could reasonably be used to identify individual students or households.
- We respect the importance of compliance with all applicable privacy laws. If additional thresholds or adjustments are required to ensure absolute privacy, we are happy to collaborate on refinements to the data as necessary.

If certain records or portions of records are exempt from disclosure, please provide a written explanation of the specific exemption(s) and release any non-exempt portions of the requested records. If this data is not centrally held, please advise which agency holds this data. Thank you for your attention to this request. If you have any questions or require further information, please feel free to contact me at request@applieddataproject.org.

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records related to the Department’s Flock Safety system:

1. All active contracts, agreements, amendments, memoranda of understanding, statements of work, purchase orders, and any associated privacy addenda, data sharing agreements, or terms of service incorporated by reference between the Department and Flock Safety, from January 1, 2017 to the date this request is processed.

2. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2017, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

3. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2017, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

4. A list of network share settings that include:
4a. Flock networks shared with me
4b. Networks that I’m sharing
4c. Date(s) when share access was granted for these networks and date of revocation of access
4d. Date(s) when the statewide lookup feature was enabled and date(s) it was disabled
4e. Date(s) when the nationwide lookup feature was enabled and date(s) it was disabled

This information may be available in the Flock Network Sharing Tool. 

To assist in accessing the audit logs, here is an excerpt from Flock Safety, Guide to Flock Safety Data for Open Records Laws (Sept. 3, 2025):
"The audit logs can be accessed via your agency's Flock admin dashboard on the "Insights" tab. These logs may show the date/time of search, user ID, and search parameters and reason. Customers may download these logs in the user interface as spreadsheets in 31-day increments. It is recommended that Flock customer support assist customers with the downloads when feasible for timeframes larger than six months and major accounts."

Please provide records in their original electronic, machine readable format. For example, please provide csv or spreadsheet files where applicable. Please do not redirect me to your transparency portal. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. title: Accurint / PSDEX Data Sharing Fields (Maplewood Police Department)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) Technical Specifications: Any Interface Control Document (ICD) or technical implementation guide that defines the data structure and data elements for the automated export of Records Management System (RMS) or Computer-Aided Dispatch (CAD) data to the "CISC Data Warehouse" or the LexisNexis "PSDEX private container".
2) Data Mapping: Any "Data Translation" guides, field-mapping tables, or schemas used to configure the transfer of local agency data into the LexisNexis Public Safety Data Exchange (PSDEX) or Virtual Crime Center (VCC) platforms.
3) Field Lists: Any record or "Schedule A" line item defining the "Database Interface" capabilities, specifically identifying which categories of information (e.g., Victim PII, Witness Contact Data, Real-Time Jail Bookings) are included in the nightly (or regular) automated feed.

Exclusion Note: We are not seeking URLs, passwords, network IP addresses, encryption keys, or sensitive security information, or contents of any data fields. We are seeking only the field name of the data elements (e.g., "Name," "DOB," "SSN," "Narrative") that have been approved for mass transfer to LexisNexis/PSDEX.

Context: We are trying to answer the question - What data does your agency contribute to LexisNexis/PSDEX? This information may sit with your organization's IT department, and may be stored in knowledge bases, emails, or other folders from when the LexisNexis/PSDEX integration took place.

title: William J. Clinton Presidential Library: WHTV Tapes of December 8th, 1999 meeting between Clinton and Ukrainian President Leonid Kuchma

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: William J. Clinton Presidential Library: WHTV Tapes of December 8th, 1999 meeting between Clinton and Ukrainian President Leonid Kuchma
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am seeking access to specific audiovisual records maintained by the Clinton Presidential Library created by the White House Communications Agency (WHCA) and White House Television (WHTV).

According to previously released copies of Clinton's schedule on Wednesday, December 8th, 1999, President Clinton held a meeting with Ukrainian President Leonid Kuchma in the Oval Office from 10:15 am to 10:45 am. While the schedule for that day notes "STILLS ONLY," photographic evidence confirms the presence of a formal photo opportunity. Standard operating procedure for WHCA during this era typically included a "WHTV Master" recording of such high-level diplomatic head-of-state meetings for archival and internal purposes.

For reference, here's a photograph from that day: 
https://photo.unian.info/photo/1348-leonid-kuchma-and-william-jefferson-bill-clinton

Specifically, I request:

Any WHCA/WHTV video recordings (including raw footage, pool spray footage, b-roll, or “photo opportunity” coverage) of:

The Oval Office meeting between President Clinton and President Kuchma.

Any associated press “spray,” press availability, or staged photo opportunity in the Oval Office or other White House locations on that date.

Any associated shot logs, tape logs, daily production logs, rundowns, or indices created by WHCA/WHTV for December 8, 1999, that reference:

"President Leonid Kuchma"

"Ukraine"

"Oval Office Meeting"

"WHTV Master" or "WHCA Video" logs for the 10:00 am – 11:00 am timeframe.

Format and Delivery:
I request that these records be provided in a digital format. If the video is located and cleared for release, I respectfully request that the Library consider uploading the digitized footage to the "ClintonLibrary42 YouTube channel" to ensure broad public access to this historical record of U.S.-Ukraine relations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

Thank you for your interest in public records of Washoe County Sheriff's Office. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the Nevada Revised Statues. Your request was received in this office on 5/13/2026 and given the reference number R033185-051326 for tracking purposes. Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

I am requesting reports in regards to any Investigations from 2015 through 2026 that were conducted as "Criminal investigations" against any Washoe County Deputy, Reno Police Officer, or Sparks Police Officer by the Washoe County Sheriff's Office 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Annemarie Grant 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2289415/ 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question up to 30 days from the date of your request. PLEASE NOTE: The Nevada Revised Statues does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. Again, thank you for using the Public Records Center. The WCSO typically processes requests in the order of receipt.  The time it takes to respond to a request will vary depending on the complexity of the request and any backlog of requests already pending.  A simple request can be processed faster than one that is complex.  Simple requests are typically more targeted and seek fewer pages of records.  Complex requests typically seek a high volume of material or require additional steps to process.  Some requests are submitted for legal review for confidentiality and privilege, which increases the time for processing. Your request will be forwarded to the relevant department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. To reduce processing time, please provide as much detail as possible regarding the specific records you are requesting. Pursuant to NRS 239.0107(1)(c), the WCSO anticipates that you will receive the documents or an update on or before 30 business days from today. 
Washoe County Sheriff's Office

Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages.
